preamble.summary: The final vision: resurrection, judgment, and sealed prophecy. Daniel 12:2 is the clearest Old Testament statement of bodily resurrection. Both Greek versions preserve this, though with interesting variations. The chapter's time references (1,290 days, 1,335 days) are consistent across traditions.
preamble.notable_variants: The OG and Theodotion both preserve the resurrection language of 12:2. Minor variants in the descriptions of the righteous shining 'like the stars.' The sealed-book motif (12:4, 9) is consistent. The numbers 1,290 and 1,335 are identical across all traditions.
preamble.structural_notes: All three versions have 13 verses. This is the conclusion of the Hebrew/Aramaic book; chapters 13-14 (Susanna and Bel/Dragon) exist only in the LXX tradition.

verses.lxx_reading: Μιχαηλ ὁ ἄρχων ὁ μέγας / Μιχαηλ ὁ ἄγγελος ὁ μέγας
verses.variant_rendering: OG: 'Michael, the great angel' / Th: 'Michael, the great prince'
variant_notes: The OG substitutes 'angel' (angelos) for 'prince' (archōn). Theodotion preserves the MT's term sar (prince/ruler). Both identify Michael as Israel's supernatural protector.

verses.mt_reading: וְרַבִּים מִיְּשֵׁנֵי אַדְמַת־עָפָר יָקִיצוּ אֵלֶּה לְחַיֵּי עוֹלָם וְאֵלֶּה לַחֲרָפוֹת לְדִרְאוֹן עוֹלָם
verses.lxx_reading: OG: πολλοὶ τῶν καθευδόντων ἐν τῷ πλάτει τῆς γῆς ἀναστήσονται / Th: πολλοὶ τῶν καθευδόντων ἐν γῆς χώματι ἐξεγερθήσονται
verses.variant_rendering: OG: 'many of those sleeping in the breadth of the earth will rise' / Th: 'many of those sleeping in the dust of the earth will be awakened'
verses.mt_rendering: many who sleep in the dust of the ground will awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame and everlasting contempt
variant_notes: Both Greek versions preserve the dual-destiny resurrection — some to life, some to shame. The OG uses 'breadth of the earth' (platei tēs gēs) instead of 'dust of the ground' (afar adamah). Theodotion's exegerthēsontai (will be raised) is used in the NT for Christ's resurrection. This is the foundational text for Jewish and Christian resurrection theology.

verses.variant_rendering: will shine as the lights of heaven
verses.mt_rendering: will shine like the brightness of the expanse
variant_notes: Both Greek versions preserve the astral imagery. Jesus alludes to this in Matthew 13:43 ('the righteous will shine like the sun').

verses.mt_reading: סְתֹם הַדְּבָרִים וַחֲתֹם הַסֵּפֶר עַד־עֵת קֵץ
verses.lxx_reading: σφράγισον τοὺς λόγους καὶ σφράγισον τὸ βιβλίον
verses.variant_rendering: seal the words and seal the book
verses.mt_rendering: shut up the words and seal the book until the time of the end
variant_notes: OG omits 'until the time of the end.' Theodotion preserves it. The sealed-book motif is inverted in Revelation 22:10 ('Do not seal up the words').
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.

verses.lxx_reading: εἰς καιρὸν καὶ καιροὺς καὶ ἥμισυ καιροῦ
verses.variant_rendering: for a time, times, and half a time
verses.mt_rendering: for a time, times, and half a time
variant_notes: Identical across all versions — the 3.5-year formula repeated from 7:25. This becomes the foundational apocalyptic time unit (cf. Revelation 11:2-3, 12:6, 14, 13:5).
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
No significant variant between the LXX/Theodotion and the MT for this verse.
1,290 days — identical across all versions. The relationship between 1,290 days, 1,335 days, and the 3.5 years of 7:25 and 12:7 is debated.
1,335 days — identical across all versions. The 45-day difference from 1,290 is unexplained in the text.

verses.mt_rendering: at the end of the days
variant_notes: Both Greek versions preserve the promise that Daniel will 'rest' (die) and then 'rise' (be resurrected) at the end of days. A fitting conclusion connecting to the resurrection theme of 12:2.
